plan:
1. Create `src/modules/zero-click-osint/map-snapshot-to-findings.ts` — a pure function that takes a `SignalSnapshot` and returns `OsintCardData[]`. This is the core inference layer. It derives ~7-8 OSINT findings from raw signals:
  - **Timezone** (high confidence): source `Intl.DateTimeFormat`, value is raw timezone string, why: narrows geographic region
  - **Language** (high confidence): source `navigator.languages`, value is primary language(s), why: reveals locale and cultural context
  - **Browser Family** (medium confidence): inferred from `navigator.platform` + `rendering.vendor`/`renderer` heuristics (e.g. 'Apple GPU' → Safari, 'Google' → Chrome), why: reveals software choices
  - **Device Class** (medium confidence): inferred from screen dimensions + DPR + touch support (mobile vs tablet vs desktop), why: reveals hardware category
  - **Screen Profile** (high confidence): source `screen` API, value is resolution + DPR formatted string, why: aids fingerprinting
  - **Rendering Engine** (high confidence): source `WEBGL_debug_renderer_info`, value is GPU renderer string, why: highly unique identifier
  - **Input Capability** (medium confidence): inferred from touch support + screen size, value is 'Touch' / 'Keyboard & Mouse' / 'Touch + Keyboard', why: reveals interaction model
  - **Approximate Region** (low confidence): inferred from timezone string (e.g. 'America/New_York' → 'Eastern North America'). Value MUST include '[Heuristic]' label. Why: geographic approximation from timezone alone is imprecise

2. Create `src/modules/zero-click-osint/infer-browser-family.ts` — small pure helper. Parses platform string and WebGL vendor/renderer to produce a browser family guess (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, Unknown). Returns `{ family: string; confidence: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' }`.

3. Create `src/modules/zero-click-osint/infer-device-class.ts` — small pure helper. Uses screen width, DPR, touch support to classify device as Mobile / Tablet / Desktop / Unknown. Returns `{ deviceClass: string; confidence: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' }`.

4. Create `src/modules/zero-click-osint/infer-region.ts` — small pure helper. Maps IANA timezone identifiers to broad geographic regions (e.g. 'America/*' → region name). Always returns low confidence. Output string must be prefixed or suffixed with '[Heuristic]' label per acceptance criteria.

5. Create `src/modules/zero-click-osint/zero-click-osint-module.ts` — the module entry point. Pattern: call `collectSnapshot()`, pass to `mapSnapshotToFindings()`, pass resulting `OsintCardData[]` to `createOsintCardList()`, wrap in a section with heading 'Zero-Click OSINT'. Export `renderZeroClickOsintModule(): HTMLElement`.
7. Update `src/app/main.ts` — import and mount `renderZeroClickOsintModule()` into `#dashboard`, alongside the existing permission-debt module.

8. Create `tests/us-020/map-snapshot-to-findings.test.ts` — test the pure mapping function with a mock `SignalSnapshot`. Verify: correct number of cards returned, each card has all required fields (title, value, source, confidence, whyItMatters), confidence labels are valid enum values, region card includes '[Heuristic]' label.
9. Create `tests/us-020/infer-browser-family.test.ts` — test browser family inference with known platform/renderer combos.
10. Create `tests/us-020/infer-device-class.test.ts` — test device class inference with various screen/touch/DPR combos.
11. Create `tests/us-020/infer-region.test.ts` — test timezone-to-region mapping, verify '[Heuristic]' label is always present.

12. Create `tests/us-020/zero-click-osint-module.test.ts` — jsdom integration test. Verify: module returns an HTMLElement, contains `.osint-card-list`, renders correct number of `.osint-card` articles, each card has `.osint-card-confidence` element, no signal collection logic in the rendering layer (separation of concerns).
13. Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to validate all acceptance criteria pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test each inference helper (browser family, device class, region) with edge cases: missing/unavailable sentinel values, unusual platform strings, extreme screen sizes
Unit test `mapSnapshotToFindings` with a complete mock snapshot — assert all 7-8 cards are returned with valid `OsintCardData` shape
Verify the region card always contains '[Heuristic]' in its value or title — this is an explicit acceptance criterion
Verify all cards have confidence labels from the valid set ('low' | 'medium' | 'high')
jsdom integration test: render module and assert correct DOM structure (`.osint-card-list` > `.osint-card` articles)
Verify separation of concerns: grep the module rendering file to confirm it does NOT directly call browser APIs — it should only consume `SignalSnapshot`
Run full suite: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test`
risks: Browser family inference from platform + WebGL renderer is inherently imprecise — user agents are deprecated, so heuristics must be conservative and clearly labeled as medium confidence
Region inference from timezone can be misleading (VPN users, manually set timezones) — must always carry '[Heuristic]' label and low confidence to avoid unsupported claims per CLAUDE.md content rules
Device class boundaries (mobile vs tablet) are fuzzy — screen width thresholds are arbitrary. Use conservative breakpoints and medium confidence
The `navigator.platform` API is deprecated in modern browsers and may return empty or generic strings — inference helpers must handle 'unknown'/'unavailable' sentinels gracefully
